title: Systems, devices, and methods for delivering and positioning magnetic anastomosis compression devices
abstract: The invention provides improved devices and techniques for minimally-invasive formation of anastomoses within the body, e.g., the gastrointestinal tract. More specifically, the invention provides a controller for providing improved deployment and positioning of magnetic compression devices at a desired target site so as to create anastomoses between tissues. The controller is configured to be used in the placement and manipulation / positioning of magnetic compression devices that include one or more guide elements, such as sutures or wires, used for the positioning and/or manipulation of the devices once delivered to the target tissue, to achieve optimal placement and anastomosis formation.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A system for forming an anastomosis comprising: a magnetic compression anastomosis device comprising: a plurality of magnetic segments that self-assemble into a geometric shape; and a plurality of individual guide elements, each of the plurality of guide elements having a distal end directly coupled to a respective one of a set of the plurality of magnetic segments; and a controller comprising a plurality of control members, each of the plurality of control members is directly coupled to a proximal end of a respective one of the plurality of guide elements, wherein each control member is configured to separately control an amount of tension applied to the respective guide element coupled thereto so as to allow for the magnetic compression anastomosis device to be manipulated in both an unassembled state and an assembled state. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the magnetic segments are coupled end-to-end to define a linear assembly having first and second ends, and the linear assembly forms the polygon or ring by spontaneously joining the first and second ends. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the magnetic segments are coupled end-to-end to define the polygon or ring, and the polygon or ring is collapsible to form a linear assembly having a length of approximately one half the perimeter of the polygon or ring. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the magnetic segments are coupled together with an exoskeleton that directs the self-assembly. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the exoskeleton is formed from a resilient material. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein at least one of the magnetic segments is joined to an immediately adjacent magnetic segment by way of a mechanical connection. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the device is configured to be delivered via at least one of a working channel of an endoscope, a trocar, cannula, catheter, or needle. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the plurality of guide elements are biodegradable. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the controller comprises a linear configuration. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein the controller comprises a rotary configuration. 11. The system of claim 1, wherein the controller comprises a constant force spring assembly configured to apply a constant force upon each of the plurality of control members. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the controller comprises at least one locking member configured to engage one of the plurality of control members and lock a position of the control member relative to the controller. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein, when in a locked position, the control member is unable to increase or decrease tension upon the respective guide element coupled thereto. 14. The system of claim 12, wherein, upon disengagement of the locking member and the control member, the control member is configured to automatically to increase tension upon the respective guide element coupled thereto based, at least in part, on the constant force applied via the constant force spring assembly. 15. The system of claim 12, wherein each control member comprises a two-part construction comprising a first portion and a second portion, each configured to move independent of one another. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the locking member is configured to engage the first portion of the control member and lock a position of the first portion of the control member relative to the controller. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the second portion of the control member is moveable relative to the first portion to thereby manually increase tension upon the respective guide element coupled thereto.
